title: Explaining my relationship with my ex wife

transcript.text: Okay, this video is talking about my relationship with my ex-wife.
Oh my god, this is hardcore.
I saw this other kid do that, what's his name, Dimitri or something like that.
And he does it, one, and I'm like, alright, well, if he does it, I'll do it too, man.
Because, you know, but, phew, no, I had a pretty intense relationship.
You know, I had a relationship with a Chilean girl from the ghetto.
So, you know, I'm from Boulder, Colorado.
Pretty different backgrounds, I would say.
I learned a lot about myself, that's for sure.
It's five years long.
Now, first of all, Chile and America's different places.
You know, they have different conflicting philosophies.
You know, Chile is kind of like, if you were to describe Chile, Chile is like the land of paradoxes.
Anything, any like extreme you say about anything, you could say that about both sides of the extreme about Chile.
You know, you name it, boom.
I could go on for a minute telling you how it's the most extreme on both ends of it.
So, it's kind of a trippy place.
Now, yeah, okay, my relationship.
Now, I was thinking about doing this video a couple days ago.
Because, or a couple, yeah, a couple days ago.
Because, like, a couple months ago, some guy writes me and he's like,
Oh, dude, so sorry, dude, you got fucked over by the chicks.
I'm like, what are you talking about?
He's like, yeah, dude, your wife, man.
Leaving you, getting pregnant with another guy's baby and leaving you.
And I was like, oh, yeah.
Because I have that written on my YouTube channel.
I don't know how to take it off.
Like, I try to edit it away, but I can't.
Like, that part down there, I don't know where I put it, how it got there.
I can't take it away.
So, I know.
And I tell people what happens like that.
Like, what happened.
And they're all like, oh, how sad.
You know, and it's not sad that she left me.
Because, you know, I kind of wanted her to leave me, you know.
Like, you know.
I mean, I guess a lot of people, it's good for different backgrounded people to get together.
Or get married, you know.
But it's difficult.
And I definitely don't want to be doing that ever again.
If I ever have a relationship again, it has to be with somebody who's, boom, is like-minded to me as freaking possible, man.
So that there's as little drama as possible.
And we can, like, bounce off of the positive and go all the way up, man.
Because the bounce is positive, positive.
And not, like, battles.
You know.
But, I mean, I'm glad, I guess, I had the relationship.
Even though, see, I get 500 bucks a month.
Oil royalties.
Texas oil royalties.
From my grandfather.
He bought up all this land in Texas, like, 100 years ago.
Sold the land, but kept the oil royalties.
So then I get the money.
Which allowed me to travel around the world.
I traveled for two years in Asia and Europe.
And then I traveled for two years in South America.
And then I was five years in Chile.
And, you know, I could have been, that five years in Chile, I could have been five years traveling, dude.
Which would have been a much better trip for me.
But what happened was, is I'm a very easily manipulated person.
And I'm like one of those people who, like, see a puppy dog on the porch and, like, would be like, ah, and then would have to take care of the puppy dog.
You know, that's kind of how I was with my wife.
Because, like, what happened was, you know, I'd never been in a relationship with a girl before.
I mean, I had a girlfriend for, like, a couple days in, like, Argentina.
You know, and I actually made a video a couple days ago where I said I never had a female friend.
Actually, that's not true.
What I meant to say was I've never had a female friend who I, like, hang out with all the time.
And, like, you know, we're, like, friends because we're, like, friends, you know.
Not, like, I didn't meet somebody else.
But, like, you know, I mean, I don't know why.
I just, I guess I didn't see it as possible for me.
So I get to Chile and I was, like, ready to have a girlfriend because I wanted to become perfect in Spanish.
I wanted to be, like, be able to think, dream, everything in Spanish, you know.
And I was 28.
I was like, okay, yeah, you know.
So then, like, my roommate's girlfriend brings this girl over, you know, three girls and the third one's my, well, who became my wife and she's this girl.
And she's, like, really cute, you know.
Like, if you see a photo of her, you know, she's, like, the cutest girl you've ever seen, you know.
I mean, really cute, you know, and soft and, you know, soft voice and everything and stuff.
And so, like, I was, like, wow, you know.

But, like, I learned a lot about things about, you know, myself and relationship because she was, like, first of all, I, if I was wiser, I mean, like, you know, if I were five being in that situation now, I definitely would have ended it right at the beginning.

Because, you know, she's kind of, like, she's kind of, like, you know, like, insulting me from the beginning and being, like, oh, we're breaking up because you didn't, because you, like, whatever, didn't call me right away when you had an argument with your landlord or whatever.
And I was, like, okay, later.
And then she's, like, so what are you doing tonight?
And I was, like, anyways, and then I get together and we're together.

And then, you know, oh, and then she's, like, I don't want to, like, talk bad things about her, but she does this thing with me where she's, like, oh, I told my family we're getting married while we're having dinner.
And I'm, like, she said that?
And she goes, yeah, so you have to go in there and tell them right now because I told them.
And I was, like, what?
And anyway, she, like, does the Jedi mind trick on me.
And I go in there and I end up with me.
I'm, like, oh, yeah, she said we're getting married.
Or I said, yeah, so we're getting married.
So why are we getting married?
And the reason the logic I had at the time was because if I got married, I'd be able to get a license to drive this car that I just bought.
And the car actually never worked.
I never got to drive.
But, you know, but I kind of wanted to stay there in Chile for a long time anyways because I wanted to teach English for myself.
I didn't work for an institute.
It just took forever to do that because it was really competitive down there.
And, you know, they have the government gives you money if you're an institute.
So it kind of makes it kind of hard to be competitive that way.
But anyways, so I was down there.
And then suddenly we get married and I'm with her.
But, you know, it was just a very turbulent relationship because it was like she was with me out of desperation because her family was really poor.
And like she was basically, I guess, got used to and then her family went really downhill financially and everything after I got together with her because her father kind of started having problems.
And so they kind of actually kind of started needing me to like pay for all the bills and everything.
And I, but I kind of, I don't know, I was kind of there, I guess.
And I like totally revamped their house.

You know, I went through like 50 bags, 100 bags, 100 pound bags of cement filling up all the holes in their house and rebuilt into a room and 50 bags of paint, paint the house and kind of made me to my little project.
But, but like, I don't know, there's just like a lot of, oh man, I'm going to run out of time.
There's a lot of, a lot of turbulence because she's, you know, she wanted me to pay more money and she didn't understand that I didn't have as much money as she thought I had.
And, and, and so we fight about that, you know, and I, and I kind of found out stuff about me.
You know, I'm a very, I'm a very fearsome person when it comes to explaining things.
You know, I will not, I will not, not explain something to somebody.
If somebody wants to argue with me about something, I will go to the end of the world to explain it to them.
You know, not angrily, but just, I will explain something because I just love to explain and talk, you know.
And, and, uh, you know, that's, you know, imagine going to bed every night and me lecturing somebody for three hours, you know.
And, and, you know, at the end of the day, she just felt like I was, you know, patronizing her, which I was.
Um, but, um, so I bring her to America and, uh, you know, but her friend is like telling me, you know, what do you think about her?
Like two times you, what would you think about that?
And I'd be like, I'd be totally awesome.
I don't care, whatever.
And she's like, you know, she's always like, you're not my soulmate, you know, William is.
And I'm like, oh yeah, cool, cool.
So, cause like, I wanted her to break up with me, but I couldn't break up with her cause I'd feel like she'd like starved to death and stuff.
And I'd, you know, I'd try to break up with her a bunch of times, dozen times.
And she'd be like, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, don't go.
And I'd be like, get on your knees and beg.
And she'd do that.
So I'd be like, all right, you know, but then she'd get pissed off at me for asking her to get on her knees and beg, you know.
And it's like, well, I mean, you want me to go or not, you know.
So, um, so anyways, uh, and she finally left me.
I was like, cool, you know, so like, you know, don't feel bad for me and stuff.
But anyways, now she won't talk to me, but I don't think that's cause she holding grudge.
I don't think she's holding grudge against me.
That's just kind of like their culture.
But, um, but, you know, maybe she does cause I was, you know, we did fight a lot.
But, um, what did I learn about myself?
I learned that I'm an arguer.
I will argue to the end of the world, but, you know, I cannot get in a relationship where there's arguing, dude.
No, no, no, no.
I will argue with somebody who I don't have to see every day.
I will argue with somebody on YouTube.
But I'm not going to fucking have a relationship where I'm arguing.
I'm going to have a relationship where there's, where there's intelligent, intelligent comments.
Conversation, people patting each other on the back all the time.
That's the kind of relationship I'm going to have from now on, you know.
And I know the guy she's with now is totally, totally, they're totally having a great time.
Great guy.
Perfect.
So, you know, at the end of the day, I wanted to, I wanted to, I wanted to go through that experience, man.
That's what I wanted.
So, there you go.
